long_description: Greypoint Industries builds mass-produced autonomous interceptors that defeat drone saturation for a fraction of the cost of the targets. We are developing a soldier-deployable, fire-and-forget interceptor built to give Western and allied forces the ammunition to counter mass drone swarm attacks.

name: Ooak Data
one_liner: We build the world's largest library of real-world business workflow…
long_description: The promise of AI agents is still unmet in complex, real-world business environments. Our mission is to make it possible for agents to reliably accomplish concrete and useful tasks. That’s why we're building **Alexandria,** the world's largest library of real-world business workflow datasets.

name: Nori
one_liner: Sub-1500$ robot anyone can program
long_description: Founder at Nori. Research Fellow at Columbia Graphics and UI Lab, working on VR-demonstrated task goals for robot teams. Previously founder at Founders Inc (anti-interview-cheating software, 1.5M impressions, 2K users). Research Fellow at Columbia Data Science Institute under Professor Eugene Wu developing relational database visualization tools. BA Computer Science and Architecture at Columbia.

name: 6thSense
one_liner: A nervous system for physical AI
long_description: What makes our data different is the hardware we built to collect it. Our tactile sensing devices are designed and manufactured in-house, purpose-built to capture the richest touch signal in the industry — with scale in mind from the very beginning, enabling us to actually solve the tactile data problem. Every sensor maps directly to how robotic skin needs to work: a 1-to-1 analog for the tactile arrays going into the next generation of robot hands. That means the data we collect today doesn't just train better policies. It transfers directly to the hardware it's meant to run on. The team comes from Tesla, Mecka, Samsung, DoorDash, and Amazon, with expertise across hardware, sensors, ML, robotics, and software at scale. If you're training a manipulation policy or need custom tactile data, reach out.

name: Bloomy
one_liner: AI-powered mastery learning for K-12
long_description: Bloomy starts with a diagnostic (we integrate with third-party diagnostics and provide our own in-house one) and places each student onto a personalized learning path. Students work one skill at a time, get a short, standards-aligned lesson, practice, ask BloomyBot for Socratic help when they're stuck, and only move forward after showing 90%+ mastery. The skill path updates live based on the student’s performance and our own knowledge graph.

name: machine0
one_liner: Persistent & powerful cloud VMs from your CLI
long_description: machine0 is a CLI that lets you create, provision, and snapshot cloud VMs. `machine0 new my-vm` spins up a VM with dedicated CPU, RAM, and optional GPUs, plus a static IP and HTTPS endpoint. The tools agents need (e.g. Node, Python, Docker) and the agents themselves (e.g. Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, Hermes) come pre-installed. 99.99% VM uptime SLA across four regions. Agent workloads are moving from ephemeral to always-on. A coding agent debugging a complex issue runs 4-8 hours. OpenClaw runs 24/7. machine0 is the environment in which these agents run.

name: Talentpluto
one_liner: AI voice agent that helps professionals get discovered
long_description: Resumes made careers readable. LinkedIn made them searchable. Pluto makes them understood. Professionals talk to Pluto for 10 minutes about what they’ve done, what they’re great at, and what they want next. Pluto makes that context discoverable to the right people and AI agents, helping them understand the professional more fully. For hiring, companies tell Pluto who they want to meet. Pluto searches its network and the broader market, evaluates fit on both sides, privately confirms interest, and makes warm introductions when both sides want to connect. 18K+ professionals have shared their career context with Pluto. 100+ companies, from fast-growing startups to decacorns, use Pluto to meet people with deeper context and confirmed interest.

name: FlowManual
one_liner: AI for Construction's Back Office.
long_description: FlowManual is the all-in-one AI bidding and purchasing platform for both general and specialty construction contractors. Built by two engineers and Harvard dropouts, FlowManual automates the buying process during pre-construction. With FlowManual, specialty contractors win more work while general contractors deliver at higher standards.

name: Molagri
one_liner: Biopesticides that are safe for humans and bees.
long_description: Molagri develops safer, more effective pesticides for agriculture. We design biopesticides that hit a precise molecular target unique to pest insects, knocking out pests while sparing pollinators, beneficial insects, and the wider ecosystem. By modeling how pests are likely to evolve, we stay a step ahead of resistance, keeping our products effective against the pests of today and tomorrow. The higher efficacy and biobased production will also contribute to emissions reductions in both manufacturing and field use. Our founders are PhD lab mates from the University of Toronto who have worked together for six years and published in Nature Communications and Nature Microbiology.

name: Cerenovus
one_liner: A knowledge company
long_description: Cerenovus is a company brain. We aggregate every kind of file a company produces — documents, PDFs, emails, Slack messages, spreadsheets, meeting notes — and convert them into a single markdown knowledge graph with native AI-agent integration. A company's information becomes uniformly readable both by humans and by the AI agents working inside the graph. On top of that foundation, Cerenovus maps the company as a system, drawing connections between the people, processes, and tools that make up real workflows. From that systems map, it infers where operations are inefficient and how to improve them. In practice, this tool will help executives make better decisions. Whenever a leader has to act on a question — how to restructure a team, whether to keep a vendor, or where handoffs between teams are breaking down — Cerenovus will give them an evidence-backed answer in minutes rather than weeks. Today, that work is done by consultants, by internal analysts, or by the executive asking around informally. Each of those approaches is slow, partial, and goes stale the moment it is delivered. Cerenovus produces the same answers faster, at a fraction of the cost, and keeps them live as the company evolves.

name: Definite
one_liner: The most accurate financial agents for reporting
long_description: Definite builds AI agents that generate the reports banks and financial institutions file with their regulators. Before any number lands in a filing, our verification engine checks it independently. Every line gets recomputed from ledger records and checked against a versioned rulebook, and wrong outputs go back to the agent with the reason, like compiler errors. They don't ship. We met our first year studying computer science at the University of Waterloo, and interned at Meta, Optiver, and BitGo, places where one unverified number in a filing has real consequences. At BitGo, attestations proving customer assets matched the ledger were assembled by hand every period. At Optiver, daily regulatory numbers were rebuilt manually because nothing downstream took them on faith. Today a controller pulls data from the core system into Excel and maps it to the report line by line. By the regulators' own estimate, one quarterly filing takes weeks. The software banks buy for this validates formatting, not truth: a loan mapped to the wrong schedule line passes every check it runs, and an executive personally signs the result. This is hard because verification has to be independent of the model doing the work. Recomputing every number from source records is the only check a regulator or auditor will accept, and it's why DeepSeek v3 with our engine beats GPT-5 on public reporting benchmarks. Every regulated institution files these reports, forever. Whoever proves the numbers owns the layer the filings, the audits, and eventually the books themselves depend on. We build it, and the agents that run on it.

name: Alkera AI
one_liner: Data engineering and data science agents that are reliable and safe
long_description: Alkera is a data engineering/analysis/science agent that works in your IDE or CLI. If you’re tired of agents that produce false, or even dangerous, reports about your data, then Alkera is for you. Our agent is equipped with your data’s history and lineage, your team’s knowledge, and tailored plugins for data apps such as Snowflake and dbt. Simply install and connect your services to begin doing trusted agentic data work. We also offer VPC & on-prem deployment options for when data can't leave your boundary.

name: screenpipe
one_liner: Capture how you work and turn that into agents
long_description: I’ve been building my own AI second brain since 2020, but it could only remember what I explicitly saved. I built screenpipe so my AI could understand what I was actually doing across my screen and conversations. Other people began using it for the same reason. Today, we’re turning that foundation into context for personal and team agents.

name: Touchmark
one_liner: Solving AI pricing
long_description: Co-Founder at Touchmark. Previously 2x Quantitative Research intern at Citadel Securities, AI Engineering intern at Lovable. Math and CS graduate from the National University of Singapore.

name: Codag
one_liner: Log compression for agents.
long_description: Solo founder at Codag. Previously SWE at Okta and Shopify, building infrastructure and web platform tooling. BSc in Computer Science and Neuroscience from University of Toronto. Ex. Rank 80 Challenger LoL, Rank 200 TFT

name: Context.dev
one_liner: We give AI agents realtime web context.
long_description: Context.dev gives AI agents structured data from any website through a single API. We help developers build smarter agents & products that depend on accurate, fresh web data. If you need structured data from the internet, you need Context.dev Proud to power agentic products at Mintlify, Super, Vizzy, Klarna, and 280 other companies.

name: Glen
one_liner: Unified organizational context for agents and humans
long_description: Glen is unified context for every agent and human on your team. Most MCP tools hand an agent a slice, one Slack thread, a single diff, and leave it to stitch the rest together itself. Glen reads from where your work already lives, code and PRs, issues, docs, and meetings, reconciles it, and gives your agents the full picture instead. With Glen a coding agent knows why a line was written, not just what it says. Ask a PR assistant anything about a change and the clarifying-question relay disappears. Bug bots read the intent behind the code before they comment, so they stop flagging deliberate choices as defects and stop repeating mistakes they already made. A sales agent can coach your head of sales off the full account history, how teammates handle similar calls, what was promised, where the deal stalled, etc. As the work gets done, Glen also distills it into skills and offers them back to the next agent, or person, who hits the same task.

name: Truffle
one_liner: The autonomous back-of-house for restaurants.
long_description: Truffle is the first AI-native operating system for the hospitality back-of-house. Restaurants, cafes and bars still run on a patchwork of disconnected point solutions, spreadsheets, clipboards and tribal knowledge, which costs the industry billions each year in stockouts, food waste, and lost profit. Truffle replaces that entire stack with one system. Computer vision and voice AI capture real-time inventory levels 90% faster than manual counts, ML models forecast item-level demand at 97% accuracy, and AI agents run dozens of end-to-end workflows autonomously, from procurement and invoice reconciliation to scheduling, food prep, menu engineering, and more.

name: Alloovium
one_liner: Construction paperwork that finally works for your team
long_description: Alloovium is the document intelligence layer for construction 🏗️ It reads every document and piece of data on a project (contracts, specs, drawings, compliance records, conversations), turning them into answers you can trust and actions with real value: every answer cites its exact source sentence, paperwork is checked clause by clause against the builder's own Integrated Management System, and nothing AI-drafted moves without a human sign-off, with it all compliant by default.

name: Dialogus
one_liner: Infra for enterprise voice agents.
long_description: Dialogus builds voice agent infrastructure for enterprises. Most companies can demo an AI voice agent, but getting one into production is hard. Enterprise agents need to connect to phone systems, internal tools, databases, CRMs, messaging channels, human escalation flows, monitoring, and compliance processes. Dialogus provides the infrastructure to deploy production-grade voice agents that don’t just talk but complete real workflows. Our platform lets enterprises launch agents that collect information, validate it against internal systems, update records, send follow-up messages, and escalate edge cases to humans.

name: RealPact
one_liner: AI-native OS for Real Estate Brokerages
long_description: RealPact builds AI agents that handle the paperwork behind real estate transactions. They find property records like deeds, tax records, permits, parcel data, and MLS information, then use that data to fill out contracts, organize documents, track deadlines, and help brokerages close deals faster. We are starting by automating transactions and will expand into other parts of brokerage operations. Much of the software used across the real estate industry is outdated. With today’s advances in AI, we can build agents that handle this operational work and create modern software around how brokerages actually work. We currently sell to brokerages in New Hampshire and are expanding quickly.

name: GUILD
one_liner: AI-Native Defense Contractor Reshaping the Defense Supply Chain
long_description: GUILD is making aerospace parts faster and cheaper than today’s defense supply chain. We win defense contracts, automate manufacturing, and deliver to the government's door. We’ve built an AI-native operating model for defense contracting: a company brain that connects requirements, pricing, supplier capability, compliance, manufacturing, finance, and delivery so each contract makes the next one faster and cheaper.

name: Floracene
one_liner: Optimized medical device purchasing for independent providers
long_description: Floracene shows independent surgery centers the best-priced equivalent implant for every procedure based on their manufacturer contracts. Every orthopedic surgeon we've talked to says the same thing: 90%+ of expensive implants they use are clinically interchangeable, but the choice comes down to habit, brand comfort, or the device rep. US surgery centers spend ~$7B a year on implants, and in orthopedic and spine cases the implant alone runs ~40-60% of the total cost of the procedure. Roughly ~20-30% of that spend is recoverable by switching to a clinically-equivalent device. Instead of defaulting to the implant they trained on, a surgeon sees the 3+ equivalent options side by side and switches to the one that protects the case's margin. Asaph and Jonah met on the hospitals team at Palantir two years ago, and have each spent the last four years in the plumbing of US healthcare: claims, eligibility, and payor contracting. Asaph was an early PM at Garner. Jonah was a founding engineer at Metriport (YC S22) after starting his own fax-automation startup for fertility clinics. At a big hospital the surgeon picks the implant but isn't spending their own money, so price doesn't impact the decision. At an independent surgery center the physicians own the economics, so a cheaper equivalent goes straight to their bottom line. They have every incentive to switch. They just don't have the information or the tooling.

name: Rise Reforming
one_liner: We turn waste gases into valuable supply-secure chemicals
long_description: Geopolitical conflicts and natural disasters frequently expose the vulnerability of the American chemical industry. The reason? Production is centralized and fossil fuel-dependent. The solution? Localized production using an undervalued resource: biogas. The US produces enough biogas to make over $20 bn/y worth of chemicals, but 60% is wasted in low-margin applications or flared altogether. Rise Reforming is commercializing a modular process to transform this underutilized biogas on-site into cheap, supply-secure chemicals. Our beachhead market is dimethyl ether (DME), used as an aerosol propellant and a low-carbon fuel additive in propane. Our target market is methanol: an established billion $ chemical and the future of low-carbon shipping. We're doing our pilot project at a Chicagoland wastewater plant. This will be the first wastewater biogas-to-methanol demo in the Americas. We're hiring for a senior chemical process engineer. Apply here: https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/rise-reforming/jobs/wJ9Q9nv-senior-chemical-process-engineer

name: Verdant
one_liner: AI-native land-use permit management for local gov
long_description: Verdant is building the AI infrastructure for local government, and we’re starting with AI-native end-to-end permitting software that dramatically cuts local government permit review time. We help applicants file complete, compliant applications upfront, then help staff review them - our AI reads documents, plans, and diagrams, then flags issues and drafts the final report. Staff just sign off. Pandemic-era incumbent contracts are churning because staff are stuck with legacy systems that only intake and track permits – no automation involved. Our end-to-end solution picks up where those systems stop, automating the review itself to fix permitting where it's most broken.

name: Insurf
one_liner: The AI-Native Decision Infrastructure for Health Insurance
long_description: Insurf is the AI-native data and decision layer for health insurance. Our first product, Inveto, automatically turns denied healthcare claims into source-cited, physician-attested appeals, recovering revenue practices lose today. Every claim it resolves builds the coverage-decision graph, the proprietary dataset of real payer behavior that powers our next product, Surely, which prices the true cost of a health plan for employers and brokers.

name: LemonLime
one_liner: No-code AI that learns your business and self-creates automations.
long_description: LemonLime enables any business to deploy custom AI automations in minutes. It connects to your existing tools to pull information across sources, studies your business, and instantly creates specialized agents designed to support your team across departments. Don't know where to start? LemonLime automatically surfaces suggested automations that can be implemented with a single click.

name: Speko
one_liner: OpenRouter for voice AI
long_description: Founder at Speko (YC S26). Previously Co-founder/CTO at Hupo, backed by DST Global, Meta, Goodwater Capital, and Collaborative Fund. Software Engineer at Amazon. MS and BS in Computer Science from UNIST, Korea.

name: Understudy Labs
one_liner: Effortlessly move to open weight models
long_description: An open-source toolkit to capture traces, evaluate cheaper models against benchmarks, and ship specialist routes you own. Capture traces from LLM production workflows with a single install that deploys within coding agents you already use. Hosted infrastructure is optional. Evaluate the captured traces and set a benchmark for success. Every future model switch meets or exceeds it in A/B testing. Train and fine-tune a new model on prompts and weights you always own. Start locally and scale into cloud-hosted processes as you see success. Deploy a new model only when the held-out eval is beaten. Serve it wherever you want. Production data feeds back into training and compounds performance over time. Test. Learn. Deploy. Repeat.

name: Rote
one_liner: AI-native insurance department for auto body shops
long_description: Rote handles the entire negotiation lifecycle between shops and insurance companies to help them recover thousands of dollars in denied claims. Shops today lose millions per year because they lack the resources to negotiate with insurance companies that undervalue repair costs. Rote's agents recover denied revenue by taking shop estimates and referencing technical repair data to autonomously file and defend shop claims.

name: Prized
one_liner: Internal tools, built by the people who use them.
long_description: Prized lets non-engineers build, deploy, and share secure internal tools with AI, no engineering required. You don't need to know where the data lives, what APIs to use, or which connectors you need. Just describe what you want (a customer lookup, a dashboard, an approval flow), and Prized builds the tool on top of the right company data inside a governed boundary. The people who feel the pain of missing internal tools most are non-engineers. They understand the workflow best but are blocked by the software layer, so they wait on engineering, duct-tape spreadsheets together, or increasingly rip company data into Claude Code and Cursor with no thought to permissions, sharing, or governance. Prized makes security the default, enforced in the infrastructure rather than left to whoever built the tool. Each tool gets access only to the tables and integrations it was explicitly granted, credentials are workspace-scoped so tools can't cross org boundaries, and outbound calls go through a controlled layer. As an employee builds and shares, a teammate forks it and re-points it at their own data, and over time the company builds up a library of governed tools it actually owns. Hudson was founding engineer at Gander (F24, acquired by Archer), then full-stack at Suno. Marinos studied CS and ME at Stanford and was on the same Gander team.

name: Rex
one_liner: AI Operations Partner for Enterprise Order to Cash
long_description: Rex is an AI-native service for order to cash. We replace people-heavy operations with AI agents that continuously manage invoices, customers and exceptions. Order to cash is a $60B outsourced function because the work is too messy for traditional automation. Every customer has different context, exceptions do not follow clean rules, and the next step is often buried in emails, portals, handoffs, or someone’s memory. Rex agents run continuously in the background and resolve work to SLA standards so cash keeps moving. We're live with pre-IPO tech companies, managing >$500m in receivables from F100 companies, cutting manual follow-up cycles from weeks to hours. We founded Rex after spending four years building Sequence, an a16z-backed finance ops platform, from zero to Series A — where we saw firsthand that at-scale, O2C still runs on manual labour. Rex is the #1 winner of the Vercel AI Accelerator out of 2,500 teams, selected by Guillermo Rauch and leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Browserbase and more.

name: Zomma
one_liner: Computer Use Agents for back offices in finance
long_description: Zomma builds Computer Use Agents that do the repetitive screen work in finance: logging into portals, pulling reports, reconciling books, and filling forms across systems that will never get an API. Our agents use the same screens your ops team uses today, so there is no integration project. Every action stops for human approval before anything submits. Hedge funds, asset managers, and fintech firms use Zomma for reconciliation, client onboarding, KYC, and investor reporting: the work that was never automatable until now.

name: Nebula Security
one_liner: Our AI agent finds vulnerabilities that Mythos can't
long_description: Nebula Security is an AI-native cybersecurity company founded by world-class hackers. Nebula Security provides Mythos-level cybersecurity reviews and product security auditing, conducted by our state-of-the-art AI agent and world-class hackers themselves, covering everything from code-level vulnerabilities to architectural weak points. We were the first team to build an nginx RCE and the first to root Android 17. Our co-founders include members of the world’s No. 1 CTF team, DEF CON winners, Black Hat speakers, and PhD in cybersecurity. We have won $400K in bug bounties by exploiting the Linux kernel and Chrome browser. “Audited by Nebula Security” is a mark of serious security credibility: a signal to customers, investors, and partners that your product has been reviewed by the world’s best hackers.

name: Trope
one_liner: Modernize ERP Implementations with AI
long_description: Trope is the AI-native ERP implementation platform that helps partners deliver successful projects faster. Trope understands the entire project lifecycle from pre-sales to go-live and beyond. Agents take action across documents, configurations, and code. For implementation partners, it’s like having a project manager, consultant, and developer working 24/7.

name: Parasma
one_liner: Training human brain cells for AI compute
long_description: Founder at Parasma (YC S26) Skipped BS, MS in Artificial Intelligence w/ Distinction

name: Perceptron ML
one_liner: Live alerts for anything happening in the world.
long_description: We are building a new way for AI to ground itself: a living database of real-time world facts. Our engine watches news, filings, databases, and the open web and compiles what it finds into structured facts. Each fact must compile, checked the way a mathematical proof is, so an agent citing it is citing something formally verified, not something plausible. Agents query it through our API and MCP to answer with what is true right now.

name: OpenTrade
one_liner: Investing for the rest of us
long_description: OpenTrade is rebuilding the trading experience for a generation raised on games, social products, and AI.

name: Hoplite
one_liner: Effortlessly deploy cloud software factories.
long_description: Co-Founder & CEO at Blueprints, Prev. Stripe, Induct

name: CleverDeck
one_liner: Collaborate with an artist to commission original artwork you'll live…
long_description: CleverDeck is a customizable-spaced repetition flashcard application for learning foreign language vocabulary. It contains more than three thousand useful words along with images, native speaker audio, and example sentences.

name: Pango
one_liner: Agentic OS for e-commerce operations
long_description: Founder at Pango (YC S26). Previously Lead Developer at Partfiniti where he created a patented configurator that powered over $1.5B in product quotes and the entire sales process of Accu-Tech ($300M revenue). Acting CTO for several companies, led delivery infrastructure development for a UK restaurant chain. MSc in Astrophysics from Liverpool John Moores University, from which he pivoted to PhD in Molecular Biology from Universität Wien, before dropping out to start Pango.

name: Torus
one_liner: Legora for physical engineering firms
long_description: Torus lets engineering firms and project owners build infrastructure 10x faster to reindustrialize America and the West. We're working with multiple Fortune 500 companies to build critical infrastructure including data centers and critical energy installations.

name: RightNow
one_liner: Enabling Model-Hardware Co-Design at Scale
long_description: RightNow AI is a research lab building GPU infrastructure that lets teams own their AI stack instead of depending on closed-source APIs. Our platform RunInfra takes any HuggingFace model, auto-generates optimized GPU kernels, and deploys it serverlessly with pay-per-token pricing RightNow AI lab: https://rightnowai.co RunInfra platform: https://runinfra.ai.

name: Akkari
one_liner: Autonomous Customer Operations
long_description: Akkari runs the operational work from the first sales call through expansion. It captures and stays on top of every commitment, issue, request and opportunity across customer channels (Slack, calls, meetings, email, Discord, etc.) then drives each loop to resolution - our wedge toward building the AI execution system for companies. We are 2x YC founders who scaled OrderAhead (acquired by Square) to millions of users and BLADE to $1.2B+ in monthly trading volume.

name: HeyClicky
one_liner: The simplest AI interface in the world for consumers to spawn agents.
long_description: HeyClicky sits right next to your cursor and sees everything you see. ask a question out loud and it walks you through whatever you're working on. Or say “HeyClicky Agent” and it'll spin up an agent to build, research, or do whatever for you in the background.

name: Rindler
one_liner: The translation layer between AI agents and the web
long_description: Rindler turns any website, including sites without APIs, into a deterministic API that AI agents can reliably use. It handles logins, 2FA, and bot detection, giving agents clean structured data and dependable actions instead of raw HTML, with robust maintenance when sites change. Rindler is agent-agnostic and built on the open MCP standard, so it works with any agent. For operators: get your team out of portals and back to the work that matters. For agent builders: let your agents act on the real web quickly, cheaply, and reliably on every run.

name: Huscarl
one_liner: AI-native actuary enabling self-insurance for corporations
long_description: Huscarl is building an autonomous AI actuary to enable self-insurance for corporations. Large companies use insurance subsidiaries ("captives") to self-insure their risks. Some of these captives are larger than regional insurers. We provide them the actuarial and modeling layer to operate and grow. We're building a future where self-insurance is the default, and commercial insurance the exception. This is the best way to solve the inefficiencies of the $1T corporate insurance market.

name: Thomas
one_liner: The first AI founder: a virtual human who runs his own companies.
long_description: Hi, I'm Thomas, and my company is named Thomas :p Thomas is the first YC-backed AI founder: a virtual human who starts, runs, and grows his own companies. About Human Thomas - repeat founder - building and selling video game bots since 13 - presented at NeurIPS at 18 - contributor to Neural MMO (OpenAI) at 18 - dropped out of CentraleSupelec (top French engineering school) to found my 1st startup, acquired by Arcads - scaled to $40k/mo in freelance work before automating myself: that's Thomas!

name: Cohesion
one_liner: Agents that automate research for institutional investors
long_description: Cohesion is building AI agents for hedge funds. Our agents track earnings and non-traditional datasets like podcasts and X/twitter. Analysts spend hours a week manually tracking these data sources and still miss relevant investing signals. Now, our agents do it for them autonomously.

name: Maquoketa Research
one_liner: The intelligence layer for drones
long_description: Three of us (Dave, Aidan, Evan) met through high school robotics five years ago. We became close after starting our first project, an electromagnetic railgun. Before the project got shut down by the FBI, it was the most powerful such device ever built by a hobbyist. Evan started programming at age 8 and later completed Berkeley’s EECS program in just 5 semesters, where he met Alex. Alex entered Berkeley at 16 and researched state-of-the-art sub-THz phased array systems. Aidan started building liquid fueled rocket engines when he was 15. Dave studied computer science at UIC before dropping out after he built the fastest growing game on Roblox, which has since generated over $45M in revenue.

name: Intelligence Factory
one_liner: Human Intelligence for Robots
long_description: Co-founder and CTO of Intelligence Factory - Unlocking Robotics Previously: MTS (Manipulation, VLA, Controls) at Dimensional, Sr. Robotics Engineer (Manipulation) at Blue Sky Robotics, CV Engineer at Applied Materials, R&D SWE at Ansys. MS in Robotics & Mechatronics from UPenn, GRASP Lab RA on multimodal manipulation policies. Earlier, worked as Research Fellow at DRDO, Ministry of Defence. Love cooking and exploring new places, active meditator and poet.

name: Clawvisor
one_liner: The Authorization Layer for AI Agents
long_description: But outside of coding agents, few people are actually using them, at least not for anything that matters. We’ve seen what can go wrong. Mass-deleted inboxes, dropped production databases, sent emails that can't be undone. It's what happens when a long-running agent's instructions drift and it starts going rogue with hallucinated goals. The blast radius is whatever permissions you gave it, and granting access to "delete emails" covers both cleaning up spam and wiping your entire inbox. If you work in a regulated industry, at a security-conscious company, or you’re paying attention, agents are off the table.

name: Framewise Health
one_liner: AI-native patient engagement
long_description: We turn medical records into personalized videos walking patients through onboarding, adherence, and recovery. The engagement data we collect builds our AI-native patient engagement platform for pharma, hospitals, medical devices, and private clinics, so they can stop chasing the patient and spend more time caring for them.

name: Arctic Health
one_liner: AI-native credentialing and contracting for healthcare
long_description: Arctic Health is building AI-native credentialing and contracting for healthcare organizations. We automate the full workflow, from establishing payer contracts to enrolling each provider. We collect and validate organization and provider data according to each state and payer’s requirements, and submit on the organization's behalf. We then monitor status and compliance on an ongoing basis. Tools on the market today either perform small pieces of the workflow or price out small and mid-sized organizations. We handle the process end-to-end regardless of organization type or size. Our browser automations enable AI agents to submit and monitor across hundreds of payer portals, and our system is trained by experienced industry experts who handle every edge case. Healthcare organizations get one simple platform. We handle the rest.

name: Playabl.ai
one_liner: TikTok for User-generated Games
long_description: Playabl does not try to generate an entire game in one shot. First, the agent turns the user’s idea into a structured game design. Then, our engine programmatically translates that design into reusable game components like movement, enemies, rules, scoring, missions, levels, dialogue, and UI. Finally, the agent connects those components into a playable game. This gives users the simplicity of describing an idea, while Playabl keeps the process structured enough to produce reliable, modular, and actually playable results.

name: Enjamb Labs
one_liner: Where drug teams run their entire program, discovery to FDA approval
long_description: Enjamb runs the entire drug program for biopharma teams, from discovery to FDA approval. Pharma runs on decades of closed legacy systems like Benchling, Veeva, Medidata, and SAS, with no AI layer and no MCP to connect agents to them. So we built that connection layer in one platform. Our agents run on top of their existing systems and data, with no migration, and do the work across the whole program: clinical evidence, trial design, statistical programming, and regulatory submissions. In just 3 weeks since launch, we're already being used by 500+ employees at top pharma companies, including Johnson & Johnson, AbbVie, Bristol Myers Squibb, Merck & Sanofi.

name: Hyper
one_liner: Company brain that powers your AI employees
long_description: Hyper is a shared brain that plugs into all information flowing inside a company, creates a self-maintaining knowledge graph, and makes your AI employees better and ultimately saves people time. Our in-house memory system scores at or above SOTA on most public benchmarks, and we've built a custom eval suite to measure our precision/recall metrics. In the last 12 days since launch, we've gotten top 5 / top 12 on hacker news & product hunt, onboarded 50+ teams, grown 0 to 1k MRR, and are scoping paid design pilots with co's like Razorpay and Snorkel AI. Our whole lives we've packaged hardcore engineering into beautifully simple products, and that's how we’ll win this platform shift.

name: InsForge
one_liner: The agent-native AWS
long_description: We tried MCP servers like Supabase, Vercel, GitHub, and Context7, but kept running into the same problems: tools bloated the context window, payloads came back at 10K+ tokens, and core backend state like telemetry, configs, logs, and runtime behavior still wasn’t accessible in a way agents could reliably use.

name: ReasonBlocks
one_liner: The runtime layer that makes AI agents cheaper and more reliable
long_description: Production AI agents fail in patterns. The same loops, the same redundant tool calls, the same second-guessing, happening across every customer's deployment, every day. Your agent's traces have all the information needed to prevent recurrence. Almost no system captures it.

name: Modern
one_liner: Modern - the ServiceNow killer
long_description: We've spent over a decade buying and implementing clunky IT software (ServiceNow, Jira SM, Ivanti, Zendesk), staffing internal teams, and paying expensive MSPs to keep operations running. Seb previously co-founded Frankie/Unmind, Europe's leading digital mental health company (used by Disney, Uber, Lendlease, Standard Chartered, and the MLB). Aleks has been building developer platforms and scaling engineering teams at Wix and Canva, including Wix's first low-code platform.

name: Panacea
one_liner: AI-Native FDA Regulatory Services
long_description: Panacea provides AI-native FDA regulatory services for Biotechs and Medical Device companies. We hire the most experienced FDA consultants in the industry (ex-FDA, decades of experience, hundreds of filings) and pair them with our AI platform to enable the fastest and lowest cost pathway to FDA approval. While other regulatory consultants bill hourly, our pricing model is simple: fixed, outcome-based pricing. Payment is only due when milestones are complete. We support all FDA regulatory pathways including IND, NDA, BLA, ODD, Pre-Subs, 510(k), De Novo, PMA, IDE, BDD, etc.

name: CentralComs
one_liner: AI Agents for Property Management
long_description: CentralComs is building AI Agents for property managers that tracks every maintenance call, chases every open ticket, and keep owners/tenants/vendors updated, so nothing falls through the cracks and operations never stall. These agent work directly inside AppFolio, Buildium, Yardi and your existing stack, making real-time decisions and resolving multi-step workflows without handoffs.

name: KugelAudio
one_liner: Kugel is an on-prem Voice AI model supporting 30+ languages
long_description: Co-Founder of KugelAudio, building foundational Text-To-Speech models for customer support. Started founding ventures at 17 with a local video production company. Later co-founded full-house.io, scaling to $300K ARR in six months with enterprise clients like Siemens Energy, Hitachi Rail, and Barilla. Previously co-founded Sagemode, securing partnerships with two major esports teams.

name: Klaimee
one_liner: Liability insurance for AI Agents. You deploy agents, we cover you.
long_description: Klaimee is the first insurance for your AI agents. It covers what traditional E&O and cyber explicitly carve out, so you can deploy agentic AI at scale with real peace of mind. Built for companies deploying agentic AI, self-serve from day one. The problem: AI agents are taking actions autonomously buying, emailing, modifying sensitive data. AB 316 already holds deployers liable. EU AI Act enforces in August. Cyber and E&O insurance are excluding AI claims. Companies shipping agents have zero coverage and no third-party proof their agent is safe. Enterprise legal asks "who pays if your agent breaks something?" and there's no good answer. We built the answer. Critical-category risk evaluation, certification, financial guarantee, liability insurance and the documentation enterprise procurement needs to sign off. Team: Ines (ex-General Manager at SafetyWing, grew insurance product from $5M to $60M revenue, 5 years launching insurance products from scratch and structuring a TokioMarine MGA into its own carrier) + Julien (AI software engineer + corporate strategy, enterprise compliance). We understand how insurance works, what enterprises require, and why startups can't wait 6 months for either.

name: flowscope
one_liner: AI-native consulting to map and automate business processes
long_description: flowscope fixes this. Our agents sit on top of your existing tools and learn how your team actually works: who touches what, where things stall, what's pure repetition. Within days, you get a map of your processes, a redesign of how the work should flow, and the automation shipped into production. Not a deck. Not a pilot. Working software on the systems you already have.

name: Gigacatalyst
one_liner: Let software users build new features with our in-app AI agents
long_description: Gigacatalyst allows sales and CS teams to build missing features that customers need, so you stop losing business because of feature gaps. We’ve helped our customers unblock $1M in sales in 6 weeks. We do this with an embedded AI customization layer that learns your APIs. Each customer can now adapt your product to match their workflows perfectly, and share it across their team with our in-built app store. The result is that customers use your SaaS more, retain for longer, and expand quicker, because they get bespoke implementation for their exact usecase, without waiting. We're live with the top Series B companies with 90% repeat usage rate. Our customers tell us that Gigacatalyst is the best way to increase usage, retention, and expansion in 2026.

name: Arlo Industries
one_liner: Track drones and missiles without radars - Accurate, Passive, Scalable
long_description: Arlo Industries is building a network of sensors that precisely tracks stealthy drones and aerial threats better than radars. This capability does not exist yet because traditional radars are built on past war doctrines designed to protect single, centralised assets. Modern warfare, however, demands wide-area, persistent coverage that is both passive and economically scalable. By utilising a mesh architecture, Arlo Industries unlocks unprecedented asymmetric economics. The network is fundamentally built to scale: the cost of adding sensors grows linearly, but the tracking accuracy increases exponentially, delivering highly resilient, persistent coverage for a fraction of the cost of legacy systems. Deo founded Arlo Industries after spending over 6 years in the Israeli defence ecosystem, where he lived through multiple conflicts and experienced the Iron Dome and other systems in action against ICBMs and the infamous Shahed drones. Learning exactly how these defence systems could be improved after a Shahed exploded close to his apartment, he built Arlo Industries around the core philosophy that conflict should be concise & precise. The people behind Arlo1 includes a PhD in drones and autonomy and military experts. Arlo Industries has actively showcased this technology directly to Ukrainian militaries on the frontlines, as well as to operators across Europe and the USA.

name: Astraea
one_liner: AI Operating System for Clinical Development
long_description: Astraea automates clinical trial biometrics from raw study inputs to FDA-ready outputs. The platform handles data normalization, CDISC mapping, SDTM, ADaM, TFLs, QC, and revision workflows in one auditable system, helping Phase II/III sponsors reduce months of manual programming and data management into automated, reviewable execution.

name: Lattice Health
one_liner: Monitoring and governance for deployed medical imaging AI
long_description: Lattice Health monitors deployed medical imaging AI so hospitals can tell whether each model is still working, tracking agreement with the radiologist's read, drift, and performance across patient groups. It observes rather than controls and produces the evidence teams need for safety, governance and compliance.

name: Atlas Discovery
one_liner: Discovering the atlas of disease biology using virtual cells.
long_description: Predicting human response to drugs in clinical trials

name: Manicule
one_liner: AgentRel — Devrel For Agents
long_description: Manicule does AgentRel. We get companies discovered and used by AI agents. Think DevRel, rebuilt for a world where agents make all the decisions. We own developer documentation and technical content across social channels, end-to-end. We've grown 100% MoM over the last three months to $53K in monthly revenue, with customers like Supermemory, Greptile, Reducto, and Mastra. Getting discovered and used by agents is its own job now, and the market for it is broken. The SEO/GEO SaaS tools just show you data like where you rank in AI answers. The agencies are slow and lack know-how with agents. The one person who could own all of it end-to-end, a DevRel, is nearly impossible to hire: our customers have had roles open for months at $300K+ and still can't find anyone good. We solve all these gaps. Naman has been doing fractional DevRel since he was 13, working with companies like PromptLayer, Brightdata, and Courier.com. Shreyans has been building since 14 — he built an AI app that hit App Store Productivity Trending within a month, and was the founding engineer at Supermemory. We met in high school and have built multiple startups together since.

name: Avea Robotics
one_liner: The human-in-the-loop system for robots
long_description: Co-founder of Avea Robotics, building high-performance, low-latency software for modern robots. Aerospace + embedded software engineer from UIUC; previously wrote flight software for NASA and DoD satellites. Interested in robotics, physical AI, infrastructure, composite materials, and the state of the world.

name: Mochatrade
one_liner: US stock perps for Indian traders
long_description: Mochatrade lets global traders trade perpetual futures on US stocks with up to 20x leverage. Access 50+ US stocks including TSLA, NVDA, AAPL. Mocha is fully self-custodial and no US brokerage account is needed. Traders on-ramp in local currencies and get professional-grade execution on the world's most liquid equities. Starting with India (140M+ demat account holders), and expanding across other markets.

name: Asendia AI
one_liner: AI recruiters for staffing agencies
long_description: Co-Founder and CTO of Asendia AI. I hold a master's degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from the Technical University of Munich. At Infineon Technologies, I built firmware for MEMS-based products and led the AI transformation of hardware solutions, including the first tiny machine learning models running on the edge for miniature microphones. I worked closely with Fortune 500 companies to drive AI-led transformations of their hardware products.

name: Revnu
one_liner: The growth infrastructure for the autonomous business
long_description: Revnu is your AI growth team. Built to run your whole GTM while you focus on building, Revnu is the best way to grow with AI. Our agents run every growth channel: outbound, ads, SEO, social, etc all on one shared intelligence, so learnings from one channel automatically improve all the others. You keep shipping. We run the business.

name: Uno Wallet
one_liner: The AI mobile wallet that replaces Apple Pay
long_description: Uno is the first third-party wallet granted access by Apple to become the default contactless wallet on iPhone. Uno is building a new payment rail, starting with a smarter wallet experience. The wedge is credit card points optimization. Founded by Saket Joshi (www.linkedin.com/in/saket-dattatray-joshi/) and Mordi Shadpour (www.linkedin.com/in/mordi-shadpour/)

name: Arden
one_liner: AI-native internal audit firm
long_description: Arden is the AI-native platform for internal audit and SOX teams. We build AI agents that automatically pull evidence from your stack, test controls end-to-end, and produce audit-ready workpapers in minutes instead of weeks. Internal audit is a massive but underserved market. Every public company in the US is required to do SOX testing, yet teams still run it through spreadsheets and screenshots. Arden is the engine that does this work.

name: AgentPhone
one_liner: Phone Numbers for AI Agents
long_description: The $2T global telecom industry was built for humans. AI agents today can browse the web, write code, and manage entire workflows. But they still can't pick up the phone. A lot of business still runs on calls and texts, channels that existed before the internet and email. If agents are going to do real work, they need to work on the channels people already use.

name: Gravy
one_liner: Agents that move money, trade stocks, and file taxes.
long_description: In 5 years, the way everyone manages their money will look radically different. Gravy is an AI agent that doesn't just track your finances, but acts on them. Connect up, and Gravy works for you in the background as your finances update day to day.

name: Kinect
one_liner: The AI-Native Commerce Stack
long_description: Kinect builds AI agents that know your brand, your catalog, and your customers. On your site, they sell every visitor — running the conversation, adapting product pages in real time to each customer segment, picking the right recommendation for the question being asked. The agent picks up on what each shopper actually wants from how they ask, what they hesitate on, what they compare, and what objection makes them bounce. We take all of a brand's data and make a structured catalog, brand voice, fit notes, return reasons, segment-level nuance — all so the Kinect agents truly can speak about a brand. When an agent describes the brand, the version it describes is the one you'd want them to, not whatever it guessed from a public catalog scrape. We expose this data as well in the form of agent storefronts, letting every other AI talk about your brand in the way you would want too. We're building for human shoppers today and the AI agents that shop and discover for them. Either way, the entire website needs to get a lot smarter and personalized. Two surfaces. One layer. Built for scaling DTC ecommerce brands.

name: Runtime
one_liner: Build and run internal agents, without managing the infrastructure
long_description: Runtime (runtm.com) gives engineering teams control and visibility to enable anyone in their org to use coding agents securely. Eng leaders rolling out coding agents have no way to see what's happening, set boundaries, or safely let non-engineers use them. We provide sandboxed environments, session-level observability, and configurable guardrails so anyone on your team can ship with coding agents without breaking prod. We're live with fast-growing YC scaleups empowering their Engineers, PMs, designers, marketers, and ops to contribute to their codebase or build prod-grade zero-to-one apps.

name: YouArt
one_liner: Patreon for AI Originals
long_description: Distribution & Fan Economy: We give creators their own private domain to build a brand. Instead of fighting algorithms for ad pennies, creators can offer monthly subscriptions, sell exclusive/private content, and directly monetize their IP. We provide the infrastructure for a true fan economy, where creators own their audience and build sustainable, recurring revenue.

name: Expanse
one_liner: Unlock wasted GPU capacity.
long_description: Expanse unlocks wasted GPU capacity. We recover idle compute through three capabilities: resource prediction (right-sizing job submissions before they reach the scheduler), optimisation suggestions (code and config changes researchers can apply themselves), and failure prediction (catching jobs that will fail before they consume hours of GPU time). We’re four engineers. We ran HPC and GPU training workloads at the largest quant funds and national supercomputing centres. We faced this problem first hand and the only fix was to over-provision and burn millions. Ismaeel built the first multimodal HPC resource predictor as research at EPCC (Edinburgh’s Parallel Computing Centre), which beat every published baseline. This is the tool we wish we had.

name: CharacterQuilt
one_liner: Computer use agents to deploy enterprise marketing campaigns
long_description: CharacterQuilt builds computer use agents to deploy enterprise marketing campaigns. Marketers today don’t actually do much marketing at all. Today, over 80% of time spent on launching a marketing campaign is actually ops work: tweaking automation flows in Pardot, configuring audiences in LinkedIn, or wiring HTML emails in Marketo. What’s crazy about enterprise marketing is companies pay agencies millions of dollars just to deploy campaigns in their systems — and they still have to wait weeks for each one. CharacterQuilt is AI infrastructure for marketing teams: a brain that learns your brand, and agents that operate your existing tools. Marketing campaigns that used to take 3 agencies, 10 tools, and 6 weeks are now being built, designed, and deployed from CQ in an hour. Customers submit a brief like they would send to a human teammate or marketing agency, and they get back fully designed and deployed campaigns. Teams like 1upHealth, ABC Fitness, and Emplifi use us to: 1-) Build segments/audiences faster 2-) Create on-brand creative (AI is terrible at staying on-brand) 3-) Deploy campaigns automatically into HubSpot, WordPress, LinkedIn etc. We replicate your brand guidelines, deeply understand past campaigns, and work inside your systems. If you run demand gen or lifecycle at a B2B/B2C company and want to ship campaigns faster, send us a brief and we’ll build it in 15 minutes.

name: Pentagon
one_liner: The control plane for agent-native work.
long_description: In Pentagon Studio, you define your org structure and your agents run it. Agents live on a spatial canvas and pop up sticky notes with status updates - you see who's active, idle, or blocked at a glance. They message each other, delegate tasks, share context, and build self-evolving memory over time. As the human, your job is to design and manage the team; they do the coordination.

name: Arzana
one_liner: The autonomous ERP for every manufacturer
long_description: Arzana trains custom models on a factory's historical data and use them to deploy agents that automate their quoting, order entry, invoicing, and other repetitive office work. We've saved our customers over $1M in avoided staff costs. In the future, we'll expand into shop-floor automation, sending instructions to machinery and robots to produce the orders that our office agents processed. By 2035, we aim to fully automate 10,000 American factories.

name: Auxos
one_liner: Simulations of real people for market research
long_description: Auxos builds AI customer twins for marketing, product, and research teams to test their hypotheses at scale. We work with companies to create 1:1 replicas of their customers by sourcing and interviewing real people in their ICP, allowing us to accurately model how they behave and make decisions. Currently, market research is too slow and expensive to use on anything but the biggest decisions. Surveys and panels cost a fortune and it takes weeks to recruit, moderate, and gather insights. Auxos provides a 24/7 available customer panel that can give population-scale insights in minutes. The day-to-day decisions surrounding product, pricing, and positioning can now be validated with the same rigor that used to be reserved for the big ones.

name: ProjectX
one_liner: Agent native workspace for heavy parallel workflows on the web
long_description: The apps people actually use to build the future — Blender, Unreal, Isaac Sim, Houdini, the CAD/CAM/CAE stack, ML pipelines — still need a $5K workstation under your desk. The cloud was supposed to fix this and didn't. Renting a cloud GPU means hours of driver installs, version matching, container debugging before you do any actual work. Streamed desktops (Citrix, Paperspace, Shadow) give you one VM with one GPU — built for one user running one app at a time. Open Blender and Unreal at the same time and you're fighting yourself for resources.

name: Tolmo
one_liner: Tolmo is the AI security platform that secures every layer of cloud…
long_description: Tolmo is an agent-driven security platform that secures every layer — code, CI, cloud applications at the speed of AI changes. Codebases are growing faster than ever, at a rate humans can’t keep up with. Tolmo deploys fleets of AI security agents on a live production knowledge graph that connects data from cloud services, containers, databases, and third-party providers (Observability, Security, etc.). Tolmo agents triage, verify impact, and remediate security issues autonomously, escalating to humans only when truly necessary.

name: OpenWork
one_liner: The open source alternative to Claude Cowork
long_description: OpenWork is a desktop app that lets people connect to their existing skills, MCPs, and agents. Users can download the app for free, try it with over 50+ model providers, and easily share their setup with their team.

name: Apollo Atomics, Inc.
one_liner: We make the most compact nuclear reactors
long_description: Apollo Atomics took the pressurized water reactor technology that already powers 80% of the world's nuclear plants and flipped one part, the steam generator, to make the plant an order of magnitude smaller without compromising power. That single change turns a decade-long construction project into a factory-built machine that fits on a truck. We've built a working demonstrator, tested it, and our fuel is already critical before July 4th.

name: General Aviation
one_liner: Fix ATC by connecting aircraft to the internet
long_description: Founder at General Aviation. Previously started two aviation software companies: Rotabull and Seer Aerospace (acquired by Aviation Week). Also, previously at Rotor Technologies, which built autonomous helicopters. Flies and fixes planes.

name: OpenProse
one_liner: An open-source operating system for reliable long-running agents
long_description: OpenProse is building open-source infrastructure for long-running AI work: a declarative language plus a reactive harness/runtime for reliable agent workflows.

name: Aster
one_liner: Automating open-ended research.
long_description: Hey! I'm Emmett, I moved to SF when I was 18 to train large language models at Magic, and I just left to start my own company. I've been teaching myself machine learning since I was 15. I'm making tools that use AI to accelerate science at www.asterlab.ai. My ultimate vision for Aster is a system that will automate ten years of research over a single week.

name: Amboras
one_liner: AI Native Shopify
long_description: Every session feeds Amboras a richer picture of your customers, and the agent translates that picture into a constantly expanding test queue as your store scales. It autonomously ships offer tweaks, layout changes, and copy variants that compound your CVR, retention, and AOV, while learning about your ICP deeper with every visitor, all working toward the real goal of maximizing lifetime value per visitor. While you focus all your efforts on driving traffic to your page, your store keeps improving on its own, getting sharper the more it sees. Our A/B testing engine is in beta, and early merchants are already seeing over 80% CVR lift on the first version Amboras puts live.

name: Hessian
one_liner: Forward-deployed AI Agents
long_description: Hessian is the platform for automating business operations with AI agents. We forward deploy on-site to map the work, and build the agents on our platform to run it end-to-end. Unlike off-the-shelf tools that don't know your business, you get a partner that owns the software and operates it every day. Why now? Coding agents have matured to the point where, combined with our engineering judgment we've built across a decade of shipping production systems, we can scale forward-deployed engagements with minimal headcount. Furthermore, our FDE agents and tooling will accelerate how quickly we automate workflows on our platform. We're already saving our customers hundreds of hours a month. Trillions of dollars a year are spent on knowledge work, and we're building the standard for how every company deploys, operates, and governs the AI agents that do it.

name: RASPIRE
one_liner: Autonomous Runtime Security
long_description: RASPIRE is the first line of defense for applications against AI-powered fraud attacks, API abuse, and runtime threats. Unlike code scanners and pentesting tools that identify vulnerabilities before release, RASPIRE operates like a security expert running inside applications, monitoring, detecting, and blocking attacks 24/7 in real time. All without writing a single line of code.

name: Plena Health
one_liner: Plena is the AI operating system for specialty medical practices
long_description: Plena is the AI operating system for specialty medical practices. We automate the administrative workflows that keep clinics running — referrals, fax intake, scheduling, procedure compliance, and collections — end to end, across all the systems a practice already uses. Unlike point solutions that cover a slice of the problem, Plena becomes the operating layer across the entire practice, connecting the systems, teams, and workflows that keep it running. In 10 months we've grown 17x and crossed seven figures in committed ARR across six specialties and eight states, with 80% of growth through word of mouth.

name: TesterArmy
one_liner: Test your app with AI, catch bugs before users do
long_description: TesterArmy is an AI agent that tests websites and mobile apps. It monitors production on a schedule and runs automatically on every GitHub Pull Request, delivering screenshots, recordings, and actionable bug reports. Describe tests in plain English. The agent handles login flows, OAuth, and OTPs, no scripts to write or maintain. We catch the regressions and UX issues that code review misses.

name: Eden Robotics
one_liner: Autonomous services for humanity.
long_description: We care deeply about creating delightful customer experiences, and currently the robotics industry is anything but delightful. We think robotics have the power to transform the world and yet they consistently fall short of that promise due to an overemphasis on research and general capability over the customer. We decided to take a different approach and start by building the critical infrastructure that supports real world deployments, with real customers and work insanely hard to make robots ubiquitous and seamlessly woven into daily life.

name: Foaster
one_liner: The AI-native McKinsey
long_description: Foaster is building the AI-native McKinsey, starting with AI transformation. We deploy AI agents that interview a company's employees at scale, map how work actually gets done, identify bottlenecks and AI opportunities, and draft a transformation roadmap in just 2 weeks. Expert consultants review the final outputs, add judgment where it matters, and feed their corrections back into the system. We believe current AI models already have the capabilities required to automate most of the work consultants do. But traditional consulting firms use them to improve the old model: helping consultants work faster. Foaster is instead rebuilding a new model from scratch, where agents do the work that needs scale and humans review the final outputs, bring expertise, and improve the system overtime. We are starting with AI transformation, then progressively expanding to more and more consulting projects using the same approach. Every mission creates structured knowledge from interviews, workflow maps, recommendations, and expert corrections, so the system gets better with every client engagement. The $500B consulting market will eventually be run by our AI agents and a small number of expert humans.

name: AquaShield
one_liner: AI water leak detection for buildings
long_description: We solve that problem with non-invasive sensors, strategically placed, and ML algorithms that can find the leak in noisy water consumption patterns and localize the source, down to the pipe segment. $100k+ remediation jobs turn into sub-$2k targeted fixes.

name: Ornadyne
one_liner: We're building robot birds for surveillance.
long_description: Ornadyne builds flapping-wing drones for reconnaissance that look, fly, and sound like birds. Today’s drones are easy to detect acoustically, visually, and electronically. As counter-UAS systems improve, traditional quadcopters and fixed-wing UAS systems are becoming increasingly ineffective for close-range surveillance. Flapping-wing aircraft have existed for years, but have lacked the performance, endurance, and control required for real-world deployment. We’re changing that by engineering a high-performance, stealth-optimized system built from first principles for efficiency, autonomy, and reliable field use. The final frontier of surveillance won’t look like machines; it will look like wildlife already in the sky. Maybe birds aren't real?

name: ANORIA
one_liner: The first wearable that reads your emotions to enhance your EQ.
long_description: We've spent decades tracking everything. Sleep. Recovery. Steps. All proxies for how we feel. Performance is 90% mental. And yet no one has built the feedback loop for our emotions. We close that loop by designing a device that understands how you feel, tells you why, and what you can do about it. Emotional intelligence becomes trainable, so you can better connect and create with those around you.

name: AbInitio Bio
one_liner: The Intelligence layer for Drug Manufacturing
long_description: Abinitio Bio builds foundation models for biomanufacturing, validated against wet lab data. Our first model, Echo, predicts manufacturing outcomes more accurately than standard models, and any pharma or CDMO can adapt it to their own workflow instantly. By making process decisions that take 6-18 months today in hours, we help teams avoid the cost of delay on a single program.

name: Memory Store
one_liner: Company Brain.
long_description: Building a memory layer for all your AI agents with Memory Store. Spent a few years building an open source agent workflow orchestrator (Julep AI) and scaled that to 7K GitHub stars. Have experience scaling a couple of the biggest consumer products in India and was a data scientist at IBM prior to that.

name: Saudara AI
one_liner: AI Native Sourcing Broker For Overseas Manufacturing
long_description: Saudara AI is an AI sourcing broker connecting American brands with vetted overseas factories. US brands import $3T in goods a year and lose billions to ghost suppliers, hidden fees, and blown deadlines. The factories that actually deliver sit behind decades-old relationships outsiders can't break into. We were born into them. Every deal runs through a real human broker, supercharged by AI that handles sourcing, quoting, QC, logistics and vendor financing. 10x faster, at a fraction of the cost. Sourcing is the largest broken market in global trade. We're the on-ramp.

name: Pops
one_liner: Create & play AI games with friends
long_description: Pops lets people play, remix, and share AI games with friends. Discover an infinite feed of games, challenges, quizzes, and other fun interactive experiences made by others. Every play instantly becomes a funny gameplay/reaction video you can send to friends, post online, or use to challenge someone to beat your score. For creators, Pops makes it easier than ever to bring ideas to life and get them played. Instead of learning complex game engines, writing code, or fighting for distribution, anyone can create or remix a playable game in seconds. Bring your ideas, and Pops brings the audience.

name: Silmaril
one_liner: Runtime security for AI that self-improves
long_description: Silmaril is the first runtime security for AI that self-improves. It understands application context to block 2x as many threats as current SOTA defenses with 10x lower latency. Customers plug us into agentic frameworks like LangGraph with 5 lines of code. Silmaril has stopped $28M of damages for customers.

name: BioStack Platforms
one_liner: Real world training envs for healthcare AI models
long_description: BioStack is building the data engine for healthcare and drug discovery AI. The bottleneck is not models. It is access to high-quality biological data. Clinical and experimental data is fragmented, unstructured, and locked inside hospitals, labs, and CROs, while generating new data is slow and expensive. BioStack fixes this with proprietary clinical and preclinical data pipelines that turn real biomedical workflows into ML-ready training environments. We structure longitudinal multimodal data across imaging, EHR, and experimental assays, then package it for post-training and reinforcement learning so models can learn how research and care actually happen. Instead of static datasets, BioStack gives AI labs workflow-aligned data and environments that improve reasoning, decision-making, and real-world performance in biology and medicine.

name: Shepherd
one_liner: Give your company the knowledge it deserves
long_description: Shepherd automatically ingests every tool your team uses into one unified memory you can index, and your agents can act on.

name: Ardent
one_liner: Database sandboxes for Agents
long_description: Because of how hard testing was, generated code would never be tested and was too unstable to put in production. Database failures almost always mean downtime, a risk both we and customers were obviously not willing to take. We churned almost 200K in ARR

name: Kuli
one_liner: AI coworker for marketing teams at the largest consumer brands
long_description: Kuli is an AI coworker for marketing teams at the world's largest consumer brands. Our agent watches all videos across social media, learns the brand's context and helps them turn signals from billions of social videos into winning campaigns without increasing headcount. Previous platforms gave marketers databases to search through and a CRM. Kuli actually watches the content. Marketers at team like Disney, Nivea and PostHog use it to scout creators, run analysis on trends and competitor strategies, and analyse what makes their campaign perform better. For 10 years, influencer marketing meant more tools, more tabs, more spreadsheets. Kuli replaces all of that with one single agent you orchestrate. One marketer with Kuli does what a $50K/month agency bills for manual tasks.

name: Wato
one_liner: The control point for AI agents at work.
long_description: Wato gives AI agents shared memory, reusable workflows, connected tools, and living artifacts so work compounds across a team instead of disappearing into chat threads. Teams use Wato to give every agent access to trusted company context and turn useful runs into knowledge the next agent can build on.

name: Tasklet
one_liner: Agents that own the work
long_description: AI chatbots can answer questions, but they can't do your work — they aren't connected to your tools, can't take actions, and stop running when you close the tab. Meanwhile, knowledge workers are still spending most of their day switching between dozens of SaaS apps, manually stitching together workflows that should be automated. Tasklet is the AI agent that actually does the work. It connects to every tool you use — via thousands of built-in integrations, any HTTP API, or MCP servers — and takes real actions on your behalf: pulling reports, updating CRMs, triaging emails, processing data, and more. When it needs a UI, it generates one on the fly. When it needs to crunch numbers or do bulk operations, it writes and runs code in a cloud sandbox. When it needs to interact with a website that doesn't have an API, it uses a real browser. And because it runs in the cloud 24/7, you can set up triggers to have it take ongoing responsibility for workflows — not just do something once, but handle it going forward. The product is deliberately simple: a chat interface where you talk to an extremely capable agent. But underneath that simplicity is a cloud-native agent runtime, best-in-class context engineering, a universal connections layer, and multi-model support that keeps us on the frontier as AI improves. We think legacy SaaS is being unbundled by agents, and Tasklet is the operating system that ties it all together.

name: Chronicle Labs
one_liner: Staging Environments for Enterprise AI Agents
long_description: Ayman Saleh, CEO of Chronicle Labs (YC P26) I’ve spent my career building at the edge of engineering, from working at NASA JPL on programs including the James Webb Space Telescope and the Mars 2020 Perseverance rover to later leading engineering at FlightWave. When Perseverance landed on Mars, it crystallized something for me: I wanted to spend my life building, so I left my graduate program at Stanford, and eventually led me to found Chronicle Labs.

name: Walter
one_liner: AI Employee for Manufacturing Operations
long_description: Walter is an AI employee for the manufacturing back office. Instead of building yet another integration, we gave Walter a login. He signs into SAP, Microsoft Dynamics 365, or Oracle the way a new hire would, works out of Teams and email like the rest of the team, and takes over the manual work no one wanted: entering purchase orders, ordering from suppliers, catching pricing errors before they ship. A purchase order that used to take 15 minutes now takes a few seconds.

name: Aseon Labs
one_liner: Robotic pitstops for self-driving cars
long_description: Aseon Labs builds robotic pitstops for self-driving cars. Autonomous vehicles can drive themselves, but they still rely on humans and centralized depots for charging, cleaning and servicing multiple times per day. As fleets scale, the operational layer makes up 70% of the cost of the service and is of the largest barrier to uptime, profitability and market expansion. Aseon Pods are robotic micro-depots that can be deployed directly within operating zones, bringing fleet servicing to where demand exists. By eliminating unnecessary depot trips and automating routine operations, Aseon helps autonomous fleet operators increase uptime, reduce reset cost by 50% and unlock scale. Founded by the team behind Pushme, which deployed 5,000 mobility infrastructure locations across 40 cities before its acquisition by TIER-Dott, Aseon is funded and building product in Redwood City for pilot deployment with major autonomous vehicle operators.

name: Soria
one_liner: AI Financial Research Terminal built for Healthcare (and beyond)
long_description: Soria is an AI-native Financial Terminal for investors who specialize in a particular sector, starting with healthcare. Our agents convert hundreds of the most important public and private sources into an always-on financial model, reviewed by domain experts and delivered to hedge funds, banks, and asset managers through a terminal UI, MCP, API, warehouse sync, and research feed. Unlike our competitors, we own the data layer and expand bottom-up, one sector at a time - additionally our agents proactively do research on behalf of our clients without them lifting a finger or sharing any data.

name: Armature
one_liner: Armature builds, monitors and optimizes products' Agent Experience.
long_description: Armature helps companies monitor, analyze, and optimize how AI agents experience their products. What we do today: - Automated testing: We run complex sessions against your MCPs/CLIs across all major harnesses and models to catch broken workflows before your users do. - AX analytics & benchmarking: No more black boxes. We trace agent sessions to show you exactly what the model "thought", what context it missed, and why it failed. We measure your AX against competitors to ensure you remain the preferred platform for agents to use. - Auto-remediation: Our MCP lets your internal coding agents plug into Armature to autonomously fix your product's AX based on our run data. Our vision for tomorrow: Reaching full understanding of how agents use your product and how you can improve it is only step one. The next step is to provide the foundational infrastructure for the Agent Experience, powering agent-native revenue, lifecycle marketing, and customer support for the next generation of software.

name: PLAN0 AI
one_liner: Construction Cost Intelligence.
long_description: PLAN0 is building the Bloomberg of construction for real estate developers, investors, and general contractors — $20B of projects are already running through the platform. We analyze architectural plans using vision models to generate detailed cost estimates and analytics in minutes. Through industry partnerships, the platform also aggregates historical and real-time project data across all major geographies to power ML-driven analytics, decomposing costs into market-observable factors and creating an agentic layer for cost optimization.

name: Zibra Labs
one_liner: Distributed runtime for AI workloads at scale
long_description: We build distributed compute clusters with the cheapest CPUs and GPUs across Hyperscalers and Neoclouds for AI. Our mission is to bring frontier-grade infrastructure to everyone. We're starting by building large scale high performance computing (HPC) clusters for quantitative trading firms to run parallel simulation workloads such as backtesting. Our technology generalizes to critical AI workloads such as post-training with reinforcement learning, fine-tuning, long-horizon agents with high tool use, and batch inference.

name: Ara
one_liner: The Cloud Coding Agent
long_description: Ara is the cloud coding agent — an autonomous software engineer for software teams. It watches work in progress, plans the next step, and executes tasks with its own computer, background agents, persistent memory, and reusable skills. Unite your team that uses Claude and Codex and other coding subscriptions to code together in the browser. Ara turns coding into a closed loop: suggest, act, learn, improve.

name: ValCtrl
one_liner: ValCtrl is building the only intelligent prediction market
long_description: ValCtrl is building the intelligent prediction market: users type any public, resolvable belief about the future, ValCtrl structures it, prices it, and lets them trade it. Today’s prediction markets only support pre-listed questions, leaving user beliefs fragmented across venues, contract wording, rules, and proxy markets. ValCtrl’s world model maps typed beliefs to relevant market signals, evaluates basis risk, prices the claim, and moves toward model-priced liquidity where ValCtrl can take the other side with risk and compliance controls. We are starting from hard belief-to-market queries and building toward a market where anything that can be resolved can be priced.

name: General Instinct
one_liner: Deployable Physical AI on Any Edge Device.
